data.title: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari Expresses Uncertainty Over December Rate Decision
data.description:
According to PANews, Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari has expressed uncertainty regarding the best course of action for the upcoming December meeting, despite not supporting the Fed's previous rate cut decision. On Thursday, Kashkari noted that the resilience of economic activity appears stronger than anticipated based on scattered evidence and data. He suggested that the Fed should have paused rate cuts during the October meeting, as subsequent data indicates that the economic situation has remained largely unchanged.

Regarding the December rate decision, Kashkari stated, "I can make a case for a rate cut based on the direction of the data, or I can argue for maintaining the current rates. We will have to wait and see." Following the September meeting, Kashkari had predicted two more rate cuts in 2025, citing a more pronounced economic slowdown at the time.

He highlighted reports of low-income borrowers facing difficulties, indicating some vulnerabilities within the labor market. However, he also noted that many companies are performing well financially and hold optimistic views for 2026.

data.title: FDIC Develops Guidelines for Tokenized Deposit Insurance
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) is formulating guidelines for tokenized deposit insurance to assist financial institutions in expanding their digital asset operations. Acting Chairman Travis Hill emphasized that the transition of deposits from the traditional financial sector to blockchain or distributed ledger systems should not alter their legal status.

Hill's remarks come amid ongoing debates about how fintech companies, which are not directly insured by the FDIC, should fully compensate consumers if their funds are compromised. Although many fintech firms collaborate with FDIC-insured banks to offer products covered by 'pass-through deposit insurance,' this protection may be challenged if a third-party partner goes bankrupt, potentially jeopardizing consumer rights. The U.S. government's deposit insurance fund serves as a cornerstone of the financial system, designed to safeguard depositors in the event of bank failures.

data.title: Fed Official Comments on Dollar's Decline and Inflation Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, Federal Reserve official Hamark stated that while the U.S. dollar is not a central topic in discussions, its weakening this year is not a cause for concern. Hamark explained that the dollar's decline is largely a correction from its previously strong position, bringing it closer to a fair valuation compared to other currencies.

Hamark emphasized the importance of maintaining restrictive interest rate policies to exert downward pressure on inflation, which remains a concern. She noted that the Federal Reserve faces challenges in balancing its dual mandate of managing inflation and employment. "Overall, I believe we need to maintain a degree of tightening to continue pressuring inflation back to our target level," she added.

data.title: Ethereum News: Can Ether Reclaim $4,000? The Critical Metrics That Will Decide ETH’s 2025 Trend
data.description: Key TakeawaysWeak onchain usage, low fees, and rising competition from other chains and upcoming altcoin ETFs are limiting ETH upside.A recovery requires stronger network activity, clearer Fusaka upgrade benefits, and renewed institutional inflows.Ether trades near $3,200 as reserve companies face shrinking incentives to accumulate more ETH.Ether (ETH) continues to struggle below the $4,000 mark it last touched on Oct. 29, with each rebound attempt fading quickly. The divergence between Ethereum’s institutional strength and Ether’s weaker price action has left traders questioning what is holding ETH back as 2025 approaches.A major driver is the slowdown in network usage. Ethereum transactions have fallen 23% over the past 30 days, while active addresses dipped 3%. Competing ecosystems — including Tron, BNB Chain and Solana — have posted double-digit growth in the same period, helped by lower fees and smoother user experiences.Because staking yield is a primary reason many investors hold ETH, declining activity and falling gas fees naturally pressure prices. Ethereum fees have dropped 88% from late-2024 peaks, reducing staking returns and weakening one of ETH’s core value propositions.1. Ethereum needs stronger onchain activity and higher feesThe slowdown in network demand is a key drag on ETH. Activity driven by memecoins and speculation earlier in the cycle has faded, reducing transaction fees and lowering staking income.Fees have fallen from $70 million per week in late 2024 to their lowest levels in over a year, putting ETH’s yield-based narrative under strain.2. The Fusaka upgrade must offer clearer benefits for ETH holdersWhile the upcoming Fusaka upgrade is expected to improve data processing through rollups, traders say there is still little clarity on how ETH holders benefit economically.Without clearer value accrual — such as fee flows, higher burn rates or improved staking dynamics — the upgrade narrative remains weak.3. ETH faces rising competition from Solana, BNB Chain, and new altcoin ETFsEthereum dominated institutional inflows in early 2024. After the U.S. spot ETH ETF launch, inflows helped fuel a 140% rally in the 100 days leading up to ETH’s $4,200 peak.But the landscape is changing. With Solana’s ETF now live and XRP, BNB and Cardano ETFs preparing for approval, institutions may begin rotating capital into alternative assets.This could divert flows away from Ethereum, especially as other networks gain traction in user activity and DApp revenue.4. Strategic reserve companies must return to accumulationETH’s drop to $3,200 has pushed strategic Ether reserve companies to trade below their net asset value (mNAV). When mNAV falls, these firms lose the incentive to issue new shares to buy more ETH — a mechanism that previously helped propel prices higher.With issuance paused, reserves may need to rely on debt or alternative strategies, reducing a key source of high-conviction ETH demand.Can Ether reclaim $4,000 before year-end?Ether still has structural advantages — dominant TVL, thriving layer-2 ecosystems and the deepest institutional liquidity outside Bitcoin. But reclaiming $4,000 depends on four critical drivers aligning:Stronger onchain activity and higher fees to boost staking yieldsClearer Fusaka upgrade value for ETH holdersSustained institutional inflows despite new altcoin ETFsRenewed accumulation from ETH reserve companiesUntil these conditions strengthen, ETH is likely to remain capped below key resistance levels — even as the broader Ethereum ecosystem continues to expand, according to Cointelegraph.

data.title: Crypto Market Suffers Sharp Selloff as Bitcoin Breaks Below $100,000
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market fell sharply as a sudden liquidity crunch rippled across major exchanges and triggered widespread leveraged liquidations. Bitcoin, which had spent months trading comfortably above six figures, slid decisively below the $100,000 mark, dragging the broader market into deep red territory.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/crypto-market-suffers-sharp-selloff-as-bitcoin-breaks-below-100000/

data.title: Trump Signs Bill Ending Longest Government Shutdown In U.S. History
data.description:
U. S. President Donald Trump signed a bill on Wednesday that formally ended the longest government shutdown in American history. President Trump Signs Bill Ending Government ShutdownTrump signed the long-awaited funding bill late on Wednesday night, a whopping 43 days after the federal government shutdown following Republicans and Democrats’ failure to disagree on key spending provisions
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/trump-signs-bill-ending-longest-government-shutdown-in-u-s-history/

data.title: Top Altcoins Slide as Market Volatility Surges ETH, SOL, LINK Lead Sharp 24-Hour Declines
data.description:
The altcoin market faced another sharp downturn over the past 24 hours, with several top assets posting deep losses as broader crypto sentiment continues to weaken. While Bitcoin’s drop below $100,000 captured headlines, the more dramatic damage appeared across altcoins, where liquidity is thinner and volatility hits harder.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/top-altcoins-slide-as-market-volatility-surges-eth-sol-link-lead-sharp-24-hour-declines/
